items.briefing:
Researchers have flagged a new attack technique where malicious browser notifications could be used to deceive Google Gemini AI users — for example, tricking someone into revealing sensitive information by disguising fake prompts as legitimate system messages. If your organisation uses Gemini, be cautious about unexpected pop-ups or notifications while working with the tool. Separately, over 170 malicious software packages were discovered in a widely used developer repository (npm), posing a supply chain risk to organisations using open-source code in their systems.

**Practical tip:** If you use AI tools like Gemini at work, treat unexpected notifications or prompts asking for credentials or approvals with the same scepticism you would an unfamiliar email.

featured.title: Sonatype flags 176 npm packages in dependency attack
featured.quote: 176 malicious npm packages exposed developer secrets, affecting Australian software development supply chains.
